entities.name: MALI
entities.description: The country in West Africa where the research study and the An Be Jigi project were conducted

entities.name: AN BE JIGI
entities.description: An eight-year research project focused on improving nutrition among rural populations in Mali

entities.name: IMPROVED NUTRITION
entities.description: The primary goal of the An Be Jigi project, aimed at enhancing the health and well-being of young children and households

entities.name: WHOLE-GRAIN PROCESSING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to process whole grains to retain nutritional value, adopted by rural Malian women

entities.name: GROUP COOKING
entities.description: Collective cooking activities (cuisines collectives) used as a method for knowledge sharing and improving nutrition

entities.name: SEMI-STRUCTURED INTERVIEWS
entities.description: A qualitative research method used to collect data from 120 women across six villages in Mali

entities.name: WHOLE GRAINS
entities.description: Grains consumed in their entirety, providing nutritional benefits when processed correctly

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: A conceptual factor influencing the choices and dietary habits of rural Malian women

entities.name: SOCIAL STIGMA
entities.description: A condition involving perceptions of laziness and poverty that limits the adoption of whole-grain foods

entities.name: ACCESS TO MILLS
entities.description: A physical and economic condition that acts as a limiting factor for the adoption of whole-grain processing

entities.name: CULTURE
entities.description: A conceptual factor that shapes the consumption habits and perceptions of rural Malian women

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A conceptual factor, including processing tools, that shapes the adoption of new nutritional practices

entities.name: KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: A conceptual factor involving the understanding of nutritional benefits and techniques

entities.name: RESOURCES
entities.description: A conceptual factor encompassing available means that shape the choices of rural women
